events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **fail**, medium risk. The backstabbed termination on a 20A circuit relies on spring push-in terminals that NEC 110.14(A)/manufacturer listings limit to 15A/14 AWG duty — even though a 15A duplex receptacle can legally sit on a 20A circuit per 210.21(B)(3), the push-in connection itself isn't rated for that continuous load, making it a latent overheating/reliability defect. Recommended action is to re-terminate with screw/pigtail connections before cover-out.
